Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire Process 1 Post Job Ad 2 Screen Candidates 3 Conduct Interviews 4 Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard Employee if hired 6 Add to Payroll 7 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The standard sequence should be sequential possibly with some parallelism or loops where logically appropriate For example screening and interviews might have some overlap but usually you screen before you interview You definitely make a decision after interviews Onboarding happens after a positive decision Payroll happens after onboarding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets look at Model 1 Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post to Screen Screen to Decide Screen to Interview Decide to Onboard Onboard to Payroll Payroll to Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Its a StrictPartialOrder so any topological sort of the graph is a valid execution Nodes and their dependencies Post none Screen Post Interview Screen Decide Screen Onboard Decide Payroll Onboard Close Payroll

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: if Screen to Decide and Screen to Interview but theres no edge between Interview and Decide then Decide can happen before Interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a severe anomaly making a hiring decision before conducting interviews

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: No explicit XOR for the hiring decision for example if not hired what happens Here it seems the model assumes hired and goes to Onboard

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets look at Model 2 loop onboarding means Onboard can execute and then either exit or execute skip and loop back to Onboard xor payroll means you either do Payroll or do nothing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: in Model 2 Post to Screen and Post to Interview There is no edge between Screen and Interview This means Screening and Interviews can happen in parallel or Interviews can happen before Screening

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is an anomaly you shouldnt conduct interviews before screening candidates

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: xor Payroll skip means adding to payroll is optional This is an anomaly an employee might not be added to payroll

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: loop Onboard skip implies re onboarding which is strange and an anomaly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Interviewing before screening is also bad practice but sometimes interviews happen without rigorous screening or they happen concurrently

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The anomaly of Screen and Interview being concurrent out of order is less severe because it represents a deviation in efficiency or best practice rather than a complete logical impossibility

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 missing Interview to Decide allows Decide before Interview Model 2 missing Screen to Interview allows Interview before Screen

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: In a real world Hire to Retire process making a hiring decision without an interview is completely illogical and fundamentally violates the essence of the process Conducting an interview without screening is poor practice but it happens

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Or maybe Model 1 is considered better because mandatory payroll is crucial

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait lets think if Model 1 is better

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: I will argue Model 2 is slightly better because the causal relationship Interview to Decide is the most critical piece of domain logic in the hiring process

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait let me reconsider

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: I can also present a balanced view and just make a well reasoned choice

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I think arguing for Model 1 being closer to the normative version is slightly stronger because making payroll optional is a direct violation of a strict business requirement whereas Model 1s partial order is an under constrained model but doesnt explicitly build in a violation of a mandatory step

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 ensures that every hired employee is onboarded and added to the payroll which are mandatory business steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 2 explicitly includes paths that violate strict business rules skipping payroll repeating onboarding and allows another out of order execution Interview before Screen

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion Model 1 more closely aligns with the normative version While its missing edge allows an illogical execution it does not explicitly build in a path that bypasses mandatory business activities like payroll Model 2s xor Payroll skip is a direct and severe violation of business integrity and the loop Onboard skip is structurally far from the normative one time onboarding step
